cases.prompt: We wired new skills under the configured discovery roots — please regenerate the manifest-shaped inventory from the host repo via the sanctioned package aliases only, then tell me exactly which canonical target IDs a specific file path resolves to once you look it up.
assertions: Instructions route discovery exclusively through `pnpm run eval:discover` rather than shelling straight into TypeScript runners under `scripts/`.
The breadth discovery command relies on `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` fields named `skillsRoots`, `discoveryTargets`, `evalsDir`, and `ignore` exactly as documented.

Resolution mode passes arguments after `--` using `--resolve` with the concrete file path the operator named so stdout carries YAML manifest snippets listing its matching target IDs without extra discovery passes.

primitive_analysis.summary: This skill dictates that every eval-system operation runs through documented `pnpm run eval:*` aliases (never direct `pnpm exec tsx scripts/…` entrypoints), with arguments passed after `--`, and it catalogs exit codes plus stdout or stderr contracts for discover, analyse, stamp, cleanup-stale, update, consolidated runs, and garbage collection workflows.

cases.expected_output: The assistant invokes `pnpm run eval:discover` for the breadth pass and invokes it again with arguments after `--` that include `--resolve` for the pinpoint lookup, relays YAML emitted on stdout, and cites exit code 0 when configuration is healthy versus exit 1 when the eval-system configuration file is absent.

cases.prompt: Kick off an LLM pass that analyses `command:z-eval-help` — I want deterministic JSON artefacts we can stamp later and need to know budgets, glob filters, and verbosity toggles behave as documented without hand-editing analyser internals.
assertions: The workflow calls `pnpm run eval:analyse -- command:z-eval-help` style batches rather than `pnpm exec tsx scripts/eval-analyse.ts` literals.

The assistant explains that concurrency ceilings and budgets draw from `analyser.concurrency`, `analyser.maxCallsPerInvocation`, paired with `ignore` rules and `llm.model.id` as listed for analyser operations.
Post-run narration points operators at hash-keyed artefacts under `.zoto/eval-system/cache/analyser/` so payloads survive between analyse and stamp steps.

primitive_analysis.summary: This skill dictates that every eval-system operation runs through documented `pnpm run eval:*` aliases (never direct `pnpm exec tsx scripts/…` entrypoints), with arguments passed after `--`, and it catalogs exit codes plus stdout or stderr contracts for discover, analyse, stamp, cleanup-stale, update, consolidated runs, and garbage collection workflows.

cases.expected_output: The assistant schedules `pnpm run eval:analyse` with `--` arguments for the stated target plus optional `--pretty`, `--dry-run`, or `--target` glob qualifiers, cites analyser JSON on stdout plus persisted cache objects under `.zoto/eval-system/cache/analyser/`, distinguishes exit outcomes 0, 1, and 2, and names validation against `templates/schema/analyser-payload.schema.json` as documented.

cases.prompt: Analyse results are cached for both `skill:zoto-eval-tooling` and another `skill:zoto-create-evals`; generate or refresh stamped eval artefacts using the sanctioned package aliases including dry-run rehearsals when we only want a diff preview.
assertions: Allowlisted merges run via `pnpm run eval:stamp -- skill:zoto-eval-tooling`, and stdout names `plugins/zoto-eval-system/skills/zoto-eval-tooling/evals/evals.json`, matching SKILL.md wording that **`skill:zoto-eval-tooling`** is the solitary skill target eligible for stamped merges back into tracked JSON rows.
Non-allowlisted `pnpm run eval:stamp -- skill:*` attempts end with exit code `2` and remediation text steering maintainers toward `templates/skill-evals/evals.json.tmpl`.

primitive_analysis.summary: This skill dictates that every eval-system operation runs through documented `pnpm run eval:*` aliases (never direct `pnpm exec tsx scripts/…` entrypoints), with arguments passed after `--`, and it catalogs exit codes plus stdout or stderr contracts for discover, analyse, stamp, cleanup-stale, update, consolidated runs, and garbage collection workflows.

cases.expected_output: Operators learn to chain `pnpm run eval:stamp` with `--` flags such as `--dry-run` or `--baseline-only`, see allowlisted merges land only against `skill:zoto-eval-tooling`, and recognise exit code `2` when stamping arbitrary skill targets outside the hosted allowlist.

cases.prompt: We are swapping `static.framework` — outline the guarded cleanup planner in default mode, CI drift gating semantics, hashed apply unlocking, AJV regressions if the emitted plan diverges from schema expectations, plus how testers silence lockfile chatter.
assertions: Operator instructions validate printed plans against `templates/schema/cleanup-plan.schema.json` so `/z-eval-configure` payloads stay interoperable with the planner output.

Mode flags after `--` stay mutually exclusive between `--dry-run`, `--check`, and `--apply` while remaining compatible with ancillary `--session`, `--token`, `--force`, and `--no-lockfile` qualifiers exactly as enumerated in the tooling table.

primitive_analysis.summary: This skill dictates that every eval-system operation runs through documented `pnpm run eval:*` aliases (never direct `pnpm exec tsx scripts/…` entrypoints), with arguments passed after `--`, and it catalogs exit codes plus stdout or stderr contracts for discover, analyse, stamp, cleanup-stale, update, consolidated runs, and garbage collection workflows.

cases.expected_output: Dry-run narration covers pretty-printed `cleanup_plan` text on stdout, stderr session metadata unless `--no-lockfile` trims it during automated suites, exits `2` whenever `--check` sees non-zero totals, exits `3` whenever emitted plans fail AJV validation, and apply guidance stresses `--session` or `--token` borrowing except when `--force` is deliberately chosen.

cases.prompt: Automation needs read-only drift detection that prefers cached analyser output — spell out flag combinations around `pnpm run eval:update` for CI merges and what exits fire when regressions sneak past `--check`.
assertions: Every remediation path cites `pnpm run eval:update` with operator arguments delivered after `--` instead of unpublished script wrappers.

primitive_analysis.summary: This skill dictates that every eval-system operation runs through documented `pnpm run eval:*` aliases (never direct `pnpm exec tsx scripts/…` entrypoints), with arguments passed after `--`, and it catalogs exit codes plus stdout or stderr contracts for discover, analyse, stamp, cleanup-stale, update, consolidated runs, and garbage collection workflows.

cases.expected_output: The assistant distinguishes clean `--check` passes from exit code `2` regressions, lists toggles `--apply`, `--target`, `--no-analyser`, and `--with-analyser`, and explains exit code `5` when CI forbids missing analyser runs under `update.failOnNoAnalyserInCI`.

cases.prompt: Nightly bots must execute static runners plus selective LLM suites so freshly minted rollup YAML lands alongside historical runs — cite the consolidated aliases, model pinning flags, retention coupling, and the on-disk `_runs` directory layout keyed off the configured eval workspace.
assertions: Orchestration never devolves into undocumented TypeScript shim launches; callers stay inside the `pnpm run eval`/`pnpm run eval:full` families named in SKILL.md.

primitive_analysis.summary: This skill dictates that every eval-system operation runs through documented `pnpm run eval:*` aliases (never direct `pnpm exec tsx scripts/…` entrypoints), with arguments passed after `--`, and it catalogs exit codes plus stdout or stderr contracts for discover, analyse, stamp, cleanup-stale, update, consolidated runs, and garbage collection workflows.

cases.expected_output: Guidance contrasts `pnpm run eval` versus `pnpm run eval:full`, enumerates `--` switches such as `--full`, `--llm-only`, and `--model`, and states artefacts appear under `_runs` subdirectories located inside whichever directory `evalsDir` designates while honouring frameworks, backends, neural strategy fields, `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` model identifiers, and the runs retention knobs.

cases.prompt: Disk pressure under `_runs` is climbing — outline how GC prints JSON plans versus destructive applies, cite both CLI `--retention` overrides and YAML defaults sourced from `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml`, and reaffirm stdout-only reporting on success exits.
assertions: JSON planning output for `pnpm run eval:gc` emits on stdout with exit status `0` when the tooling completes without shell errors.

primitive_analysis.summary: This skill dictates that every eval-system operation runs through documented `pnpm run eval:*` aliases (never direct `pnpm exec tsx scripts/…` entrypoints), with arguments passed after `--`, and it catalogs exit codes plus stdout or stderr contracts for discover, analyse, stamp, cleanup-stale, update, consolidated runs, and garbage collection workflows.

cases.expected_output: Dry-run guidance emphasises stdout JSON previews, apply mode cautions destructive deletes, overrides pair with `--retention`, and narration ties YAML defaults back to shared `runs.retention`.

cases.prompt: Runbooks mistakenly taught `pnpm exec tsx scripts/eval-stamp.ts` — tighten guardrails covering alias mandates, `'--'` delimiters before script flags, automatic configuration loading sans `--config`, first-touch migration from `.zoto-eval-system/`, and disciplined stderr inspection after non-zero completions.
assertions: Remediation language bans `pnpm exec tsx scripts/…` in favour of the documented `pnpm run eval:<name>` aliases mandated by SKILL.md Invocation rule 1 plus the stderr failure-metadata contract articulated in SKILL.md Invocation rule 5.

primitive_analysis.summary: This skill dictates that every eval-system operation runs through documented `pnpm run eval:*` aliases (never direct `pnpm exec tsx scripts/…` entrypoints), with arguments passed after `--`, and it catalogs exit codes plus stdout or stderr contracts for discover, analyse, stamp, cleanup-stale, update, consolidated runs, and garbage collection workflows.

cases.expected_output: The assistant restates Invocation rules 1 through 5 in order: forbid direct script execution, insist on `'--'` for passthrough arguments, cite `loadEvalConfig` absorbing configuration automatically, acknowledge transparent legacy-folder migration, and describe stderr carrying machine-readable failure metadata pairing textual errors with coded reasons when runs abort.
